ogTitle: Stirling Range National Park vs Stove Hill: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Stirling Range National Park and Stove Hill.
heading: Stirling Range National Park vs Stove Hill — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Stirling Range National Park, Western Australia and Stove Hill, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Stirling Range National Park has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Stove Hill (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Stirling Range National Park or Stove Hill safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Stirling Range National Park has a flood risk score of 35/100, while Stove Hill scores 53/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Stirling Range National Park scores 59/100 for bushfire risk. Stove Hill scores 56/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Stirling Range National Park or Stove Hill?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Stirling Range National Park has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
